title: austrostipa rudis subsp. nervosa
intro: Austrostipa rudis subsp. nervosa, also known as nervous blady grass, is a type of grass native to eastern Australia, characterized by its tall, slender stems and feathery leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Austrostipa rudis subsp. nervosa include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to apply its medicinal properties topically or internally.
